raw_consumer_statement:
Transaction Date, XX/XX/XXXX BestBuy Co., XXXX XXXX, IL Amount, {$3400.00} XX/XX/XXXX, we entered a contract with Best Buy to purchase and have installed a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Dual Combo Oven identified as an OPEN ITEM in EXCELLENT condition. Order # XXXX. 

XX/XX/XXXX that item was installed. 
*****At the same time our fully functioning XXXX double-oven was removed by the Best Buy contractor. ***** XX/XX/XXXX we were informed by XXXX XXXX customer support that my new oven had been declared " dangerous '' and a field destroy in XXXX of XXXX in XXXX, Indiana, and as such is ineligible for support. Over XXXX XXXX miles away in a different state more than XXXX  months before we purchased this same unit. with NO disclosure on it's status other " New '', " Excellent Condition '', and an " Open Box ''. 

XX/XX/XXXX the Best Buy store manager offered the following resolution options : XXXX. Return the unit. 
XXXX. Return the unit and buy an alternate. 
XXXX. Keep the unit and assume the risk that it will fail. 
XXXX. That the additional extended warranty we purchased would not cover this oven. 
These options fail to satisfy the original contract and place all financial and risk burdens on me. When we asked to escalate our concerns, we were advised that that was not possible. 

Since then, we spent over XXXX documented hours on the phone with BestBuy. 

Having once reached XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ), who identified himself as a manager agreed that a service technician be dispatched. The tech identified a multitude of product failures and took no action. Subsequently, XXXX has failed to answer or return our calls. 

All interactions with Best Buy Customer Service have resulted in the same, unacceptable resolution option offered by the store manager. 

My complaint has been filed with Best Buy customer service. Case : XXXX. 
A dispute has been filed with My Best Buy XXXX. Case ID : XXXX.
